title: Source-vetting + provenance protocol (primary-source citation, reuse-terms verification, lastVerified/validUntil)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task defines the source-vetting and provenance protocol - primary-source citation, reuse-terms verification, and lastVerified/validUntil staleness fields - so every program fact is traceable to a license-clean primary source and can be re-verified on a cadence.
objective: Define the source-vetting + provenance protocol governing primary-source citation, reuse-terms verification, and the lastVerified/validUntil staleness fields used by all sourced content.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines a primary-source citation requirement and the no-source-no-claim rule for every program fact, threshold, and figure

Specifies reuse-terms verification per source: federal works treated as public domain (17 U.S.C. 105, cited not copied); every non-federal/state source's reuse terms verified and recorded before enabling

Defines the recorded provenance fields per source (name, jurisdiction, citation/section, retrieval date, URL, reuse-status note, lastVerified, validUntil) and the expert sign-off gate before sources are enabled
